d7b_reasoning: The strategy implements a coherent mean reversion logic: entering when price crosses below Bollinger band (though the condition uses bb_upper_24_2 instead of bb_lower), RSI indicates oversold (< 35), zscore confirms statistical deviation (< -1.5), and return_24h shows downward momentum (< -2%). Exit logic is sensible, triggering when price crosses above sma_24 with RSI recovering above 55, or via quick profit-taking at 2.5% gain within 72-bar maximum hold. Theme alignment is strong, utilizing volatility bands, z-scores, and moving averages directly from hint factors. However, structural variant risk is very high: this factor set {bb_upper_24_2, close, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_24, zscore_48} is nearly identical to prior #43 which used {bb_upper_24_2, close, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14, zscore_48} and #29 which used {bb_upper_24_2, close, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_24, zscore_48}. The current hypothesis merely combines elements from these two, differing only in threshold values (RSI 35 vs typical 30, zscore -1.5 vs -2.0). This represents a shallow parameter adjustment rather than a structurally novel approach.
